questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with the Fundamental Rights of Indian citizens?
questions.explanation: It includes rights such as equality, freedom of speech, and right to life.

questions.question: What is the concept of Panchayati Raj mentioned in the Indian Constitution?
options: A system of direct democracy at the local level
A system of indirect democracy at the state level
A system of direct democracy at the state level
A system of indirect democracy at the local level
questions.explanation: It is a system of local self-government.

questions.question: The West Bengal Panchayat (Amendment) Act, 2020 was aimed at strengthening the __________ in the state?
questions.explanation: The amendment aimed to increase their powers and representation.

questions.question: According to the Indian Constitution, which of the following is a fundamental duty of every citizen?
options: To respect the Constitution and the national flag
To abide by the laws of the land
To pay taxes to the government
questions.explanation: It is mentioned in Article 51A of the Constitution.

questions.question: The 73rd Amendment to the Indian Constitution was enacted to give constitutional status to the __________?
questions.explanation: It aimed to empower the grassroots level of local self-government.

questions.question: Which river originates from the Himalayas and flows through the Siliguri Corridor?
questions.explanation: The Tista River originates from the Himalayas.

questions.question: Which of the following cities is the administrative headquarters of the Sundarbans region?
questions.explanation: Godkhali is the administrative headquarters of the Sundarbans region.

questions.question: What is the name of the mountain peak in the Darjeeling Himalayan hill region?
questions.explanation: Kangchenjunga is the highest peak in the Darjeeling Himalayan hill region.

questions.question: Which of the following districts in West Bengal is known as the 'Land of the Singhal Singhs'?
questions.explanation: Birbhum is known as the 'Land of the Singhal Singhs'.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest river delta in the world located in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Ganga-Brahmaputra Delta is the largest river delta in the world located in West Bengal.

questions.question: Under which scheme is the West Bengal government providing 5 kg of rice and 1 kg of lentils per family member to the BPL families?
questions.explanation: The Anandamaya Scheme is a food security initiative.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is a health insurance initiative by the West Bengal government?
questions.explanation: The Swasthya Sathi Scheme provides health insurance.

questions.question: The West Bengal government has launched which scheme for the welfare of girls?
questions.explanation: The Kanyashree Prakalpa aims to empower girls.

questions.question: Which of the following is a flagship scheme of the West Bengal government for the education sector?
questions.explanation: The Sikshashree Scheme provides financial assistance to students.

questions.question: The Sabuj Sathi Scheme is aimed at providing which of the following to students in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Sabuj Sathi Scheme provides bicycles to students.

questions.question: If a person buys an article marked Rs. 400 at a discount of 15% and sells it at a profit of 20%, what is his gain percentage?
questions.explanation: Gain percentage is calculated on the cost price.

questions.question: A shopkeeper marks an article at 25% above the cost price and then offers a discount of 10%. If a customer buys the article at Rs. 1,120, what is his cost price?
questions.explanation: The selling price is 90% of the marked price.

questions.question: A person buys 200 shares of Rs. 10 each at Rs. 12 per share. He sells 50 shares at a 10% profit. What is his profit percentage on the sale of these shares?
questions.explanation: Profit percentage is calculated on the cost price of the shares sold.

questions.question: A man invests Rs. 50,000 in a business and gets a profit of 12%. He then withdraws Rs. 20,000 and gets a profit of 15% on the remaining amount. What is his total profit?
questions.explanation: Profit is calculated on the investment and the profit percentages.

questions.question: If a merchant sells a watch at a profit of 8% and then gives a discount of 2%, what is the overall profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Overall profit percentage is calculated after considering both profit and discount.

questions.question: A person can complete 1/4th of a certain work in 10 days. How long will it take for the same person to complete 60% of the same work?
questions.explanation: Work is directly proportional to the time taken.

questions.question: A pipe can fill 1/3rd of a tank in 15 minutes. How long will it take to fill 7/8th of the same tank?
questions.explanation: Proportionate time is taken to fill the tank.

questions.question: 3 persons can complete a piece of work in 6 days. How many persons will be required to complete the same work in 6 days?
questions.explanation: Work is inversely proportional to the number of persons.

questions.question: A tap can fill 1/4th of a tank in 24 minutes. How long will it take to fill 5/8th of the same tank?
questions.explanation: Proportionate time is taken to fill the tank.

questions.question: If 10 men can reap 60 acres in 8 days, then how many men will be required to reap 90 acres in 10 days?
questions.explanation: Work is directly proportional to the number of men.

questions.question: A train travels from Howrah to Sealdah at a speed of 60 km/h. If the distance between the two stations is 20 km, what is the time taken by the train to cover the distance?
questions.explanation: Time taken is 1.5 hours as distance = speed x time.

questions.question: A car travels from Kolkata to Digha at an average speed of 80 km/h. If the distance between the two places is 240 km, how many hours will it take to cover the distance?
questions.explanation: Time taken is 3 hours as time = distance/speed.

questions.question: A bus travels from Esplanade to Gariahat in 30 minutes at a speed of 40 km/h. What is the distance between the two places?
questions.explanation: Distance is 8 km as distance = speed x time.

questions.question: If a man walks at a speed of 5 km/h, he covers a distance of 20 km in 4 hours. What is the time taken to cover a distance of 30 km at the same speed?
questions.explanation: Time taken is 6 hours as time = distance/speed.

questions.question: A car starts from Howrah at 9:00 AM and reaches Digha at 2:00 PM. If the distance between the two places is 240 km, what is the average speed of the car?
questions.explanation: Average speed is 60 km/h as distance = speed x time.

questions.question: In the given series 3, 6, 12, 24, 48, ..., what comes next?
questions.explanation: The series is a geometric progression with a common ratio of 2.

questions.question: A code is given as: A@B=C, B#D=E, C$F=G, What is the code for 'K'?
questions.explanation: The code is a simple substitution code where '@' represents 'A', '#' represents 'B', and so on.

questions.question: If the code is: a2b3c1, what is the code for 'abc'?
questions.explanation: The code follows a simple pattern where each letter is followed by its position in the alphabet.

questions.question: A code is given as: Howrah=H, Kolkata=K, Bidhan nagar=B, What is the code for 'Siliguri'?
questions.explanation: The code is a simple substitution code where the first letter of each city is used as the code.

questions.question: In the given series 5, 10, 20, 35, 55, ..., what comes next?
questions.explanation: The series is a sequence of numbers where each number is the sum of the previous number and a consecutive integer.

questions.question: Identify the brother of Kishore, whose mother is the wife of the brother of father's first wife?
questions.explanation: By analyzing the given information, Rahul is identified as the brother of Kishore.

questions.question: If A is the only son of B, C is the brother of D, and E is the daughter of C, then who is the father of E?
questions.explanation: E's father is identified as C, as E is the daughter of C.

questions.question: A is the daughter of B, C is the wife of B, and D is the son of C. Who is the mother of D?
questions.explanation: D's mother is identified as C, as D is the son of C.

questions.question: If A is the father of B, C is the son of A, and D is the brother of C, then who is the grandfather of D?
questions.explanation: D's grandfather is identified as A, as D is the brother of C, and C is the son of A.

questions.question: A is the mother of B, C is the sister of B, and D is the daughter of A. Who is the aunt of D?
questions.explanation: D's aunt is identified as C, as D is the daughter of A, and C is the sister of B, who is the daughter of A.

questions.question: A person is standing at the bank of Ganges in Howrah, facing towards the river. He then turns 180 degrees to his left and walks for 20 meters. Then he turns 90 degrees to his right and walks for 15 meters. Finally, he turns 270 degrees to his left and walks for 20 meters. In which direction is he facing now?
questions.explanation: The person ends up facing the original direction.

questions.question: A man is standing at the Dakshineshwar Kali Temple in Kolkata. He is facing towards the Kali Maa temple. If he turns 135 degrees to his left, which direction is he facing now?
questions.explanation: He will be facing South West direction.

questions.question: A person is standing at the Park Circus area in Kolkata. He is facing towards the North. He then walks 50 meters to his right. Then he walks 20 meters to his left. Then he walks 30 meters to his right. Finally, he walks 10 meters to his left. Which direction is he facing now?
questions.explanation: He is still facing North direction.

questions.question: A person is standing at the Esplanade area in Kolkata. He is facing towards the Victoria Memorial Hall. If he turns 90 degrees to his left, which direction is he facing now?
questions.explanation: He will be facing North direction.

questions.question: A person is walking from the Dakshineshwar to the Belur Math. He is facing the direction of Belur Math. He then turns 180 degrees to his left. Which direction is he facing now?
questions.explanation: He is facing away from Belur Math.
